text:
Market today: Mixed. Rising Treasury yields are a headwind for your growth-heavy tech/chip portfolio, while strategists are questioning the dominance of the 'Magnificent Seven.' However, there's continued positive news in the chip sector with AI investments.

Your holdings: SOXL (-46.77%) and SPAL (-47.83%) are in deep drawdowns, compounded by their leveraged nature and decaying value. Their current stops are ineffective. SMH (-12.55%) and QQQ (-2.7%) are also flagged as SELL and nearing their stops. Margin amplifies all these drops.

Watch: Keep an eye on Treasury yields for continued upward pressure on tech valuations. Also, monitor any shifts away from mega-cap tech as strategists suggest rotating out of the 'Magnificent Seven.'

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your leveraged, underwater positions to protect your capital.
plays: SOXL: Your stop at 200 is useless with price at 135.47 and a -46.77% loss. Re-evaluate this leveraged, decaying position immediately.
SPAL: This new leveraged ETF is down -47.83% at 13.12, with a stop at 21.38. Consider cutting losses on this volatile, decaying position.
SMH: Watch your stop at 540 closely. With SMH at 556.53 and a -12.55% loss, hitting this stop would mean trimming further chip exposure.
